title: Bhakti-Yoga: the Most Auspicious way to Conquer Death
summary: Krishna teaches Uddhava about witness consciousness, detachment from family/objects, and devotion (Bhakti). Addresses relationship endings, dealing with grief, letting go of attachments, and mental calm.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “S'r Uddhava said: 'This process of yoga is, I think, most difficult to execute for someone not spiritual. Please, oh Acyuta, tell me in simple terms how a person may easily succeed [see…” and develops toward “My dear, because this by Me perfectly established method is free from the modes and has no ulterior motives, there is, when one thus tries to be of…”

This passage begins with “Oh best among the pious souls, when one is capable of performing one's duty free from ulterior motives for the sake of Me, the One Supreme, [the emotions] of that endeavor like fear and…” and develops toward “Let me offer my obeisances unto You, oh Greatest Yogi, please tell me how I as a surrendered soul can be steadfast in the attraction of Your lotus…”

This passage begins with “The Supreme Lord said: 'Please Uddhava, accept My advice to head for My hermitage called Badrika. At the riverbanks there be purified by the touching of and bathing in the water emanating…” and develops toward “I am bowed down to the greatest and first of all beings, the personality named Krishna, who makes His many devotees drink the nectar from the [milk]…”

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच सु-दुस्तराम् इमां मन्ये योग-चर्याम् अनात्मनः । यथाञ्जसा पुमान् सिद्ध्येत् तन् मे ब्रूह्य् अञ्जसाच्युत
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca su-dustarām imāṃ manye yoga-caryām anātmanaḥ / yathāñjasā pumān siddhyet tan me brūhy añjasācyuta
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'This process of yoga is, I think, most difficult to execute for someone not spiritual. Please, oh Acyuta, tell me in simple terms how a person may easily succeed [see also B.G. 6: 33-34].

verses.sanskrit: प्रायशः पुण्दरीकाक्ष युञ्यन्तो योगिनो मनः । विषीदन्त्य् असमाधानान् मनो-निग्रह-कर्शिताः
verses.transliteration: prāyaśaḥ puṇdarīkākṣa yuñyanto yogino manaḥ / viṣīdanty asamādhānān mano-nigraha-karśitāḥ
verses.translation: Generally, oh Lotus-eyed One, [beginning] yoga practitioners get frustrated trying to unite the mind and grow weary of subduing their thoughts being unable to find absorption.

verses.sanskrit: अथात आनन्द-दुघं पदाम्बुजं हंसाः श्रयेरन्न् अरविन्द-लोचन । सुखं नु विश्वेश्वर योग-कर्मभिस् त्वन्-माययामी विहता न मानिनः
verses.transliteration: athāta ānanda-dughaṃ padāmbujaṃ haṃsāḥ śrayerann aravinda-locana / sukhaṃ nu viśveśvara yoga-karmabhis tvan-māyayāmī vihatā na māninaḥ
verses.translation: For that reason, oh Lotus-eyed Lord of the Universe, the swanlike [devotees] delight in taking to the shelter of Your lotus feet that are the source of all ecstasy, while they who take pride in the results of their yoga, do not [take shelter] and are defeated by Your material energy.

verses.sanskrit: किं चित्रम् अच्युत तवैतद् अशेष-बन्धो दासेष्व् अनन्य-शरणेसु यद् आत्म-सात्त्वम् । यो ऽरोचयत् सह मृगैः स्वयम् ईश्वराणां श्रीमत्-किरीट-तट-पीडित-पाद-पीठः
verses.transliteration: kiṃ citram acyuta tavaitad aśeṣa-bandho dāseṣv ananya-śaraṇesu yad ātma-sāttvam / yo 'rocayat saha mṛgaiḥ svayam īśvarāṇāṃ śrīmat-kirīṭa-taṭa-pīḍita-pāda-pīṭhaḥ
verses.translation: It comes as no surprise Acyuta, that You as a friend to all servants with no other shelter, are joined in intimacy with them [are commanded by them], You who [as Rma] were affectionate with the animal-like [Vnaras] while the edges of Your footstool were covered by the effulgen t helmets of the great controllers [like Brahm].

verses.sanskrit: तं त्वाखिलात्म-दयितेश्वरम् आश्रितानां सर्वार्थ-दं स्व-कृत-विद् विसृजेत को नु को वा भजेत् किम् अपि विस्मृतये ऽनु भूत्यै किं वा भवेन् न तव पाद-रजो-जुषां नः
verses.transliteration: taṃ tvākhilātma-dayiteśvaram āśritānāṃ $ sarvārtha-daṃ sva-kṛta-vid visṛjeta ko nu &ko vā bhajet kim api vismṛtaye 'nu bhūtyai % kiṃ vā bhaven na tava pāda-rajo-juṣāṃ naḥ
verses.translation: Knowing the benefit You offer, oh Supreme Soul, Bestower of All Perfections and dearest Lord to those seeking shelter, who would reject You or ever be devoted to anything else and forget [about You in exchange] for some opulence? What would not be granted to those serving the dust of Your feet [see also 10.44: 15, 10.47: 46]?

verses.sanskrit: नैवोपयन्त्य् अपचितिं कवयस् तवेश ब्रह्मायुषापि कृतम् ऋद्ध-मुदः स्मरन्तः नैवोपयन्त्य् अपचितिं कवयस् तवेश ब्रह्मायुषापि कृतम् ऋद्ध-मुदः स्मरन्तः यो ऽन्तर् बहिस् तनु-भृताम् अशुभं विधुन्वन्न् आचार्य-चैत्त्य-वपुषा स्व-गतिं व्यनक्ति
verses.transliteration: naivopayanty apacitiṃ kavayas taveśa $ brahmāyuṣāpi kṛtam ṛddha-mudaḥ smarantaḥ &naivopayanty apacitiṃ kavayas taveśa $ brahmāyuṣāpi kṛtam ṛddha-mudaḥ smarantaḥ &yo 'ntar bahis tanu-bhṛtām aśubhaṃ vidhunvann % ācārya-caittya-vapuṣā sva-gatiṃ vyanakti
verses.translation: The scholars - despite all their work - would not even with a lifetime as long as Brahm's be capable of expressing the gratitude [we owe You], oh Lord. For You, in order to remind us of the greater [spiritual] joy of dispelling the sadness of being embodied, show us Your path in two ways: in the form of that what from the caittya authority [of the Supersoul] is mentally conceived within and that what from the outside is conceived on the authority of the crya [the parampar-guru of the tradition].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-शुक उवाच इत्य् उद्धवेनात्य्-अनुरक्त-चेतसा पृष्टो जगत्-क्रीडनकः स्व-शक्तिभिः । गृहीत-मूर्ति-त्रय ईश्वरेश्वरो जगाद स-प्रेम-मनोहर-स्मितः
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvāca ity uddhavenāty-anurakta-cetasā pṛṣṭo jagat-krīḍanakaḥ sva-śaktibhiḥ / gṛhīta-mūrti-traya īśvareśvaro jagāda sa-prema-manohara-smitaḥ
verses.translation: S'r S'uka said: 'Thus questioned by Uddhava who in his heart was most attached to Him, the Lord of all Lords spoke lovingly with an attractive smile, He who - with the universe as His plaything - by His energies assumed His three [principal] forms [the guna-avatras].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि मम धर्मान् सु-मङ्गलान् । यान् श्रद्धयाचरन् मर्त्यो मृत्युं जयति दुर्जयम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca hanta te kathayiṣyāmi mama dharmān su-maṅgalān / yān śraddhayācaran martyo mṛtyuṃ jayati durjayam
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'I shall explain to you My most auspicious dharma by means of which, with faith executed, a mortal being may defeat unconquerable death.

verses.sanskrit: कुर्यात् सर्वाणि कर्माणि मद्-अर्थं शनकैः स्मरन् । मय्य् अर्पित-मनश्-चित्तो मद्-धर्मात्म-मनो-रतिः
verses.transliteration: kuryāt sarvāṇi karmāṇi mad-arthaṃ śanakaiḥ smaran / mayy arpita-manaś-citto mad-dharmātma-mano-ratiḥ
verses.translation: He whose mind is attracted to My devotional service and has offered his heart and intelligence unto Me, should remember to perform, step by step, all his prescribed duties for My sake.

verses.sanskrit: देशान् पुण्यान् आश्रयेत मद्-भक्तैः साधुभिः श्रितान् । देवासुर-मनुष्येषु मद्-भक्ताचरितानि च
verses.transliteration: deśān puṇyān āśrayeta mad-bhaktaiḥ sādhubhiḥ śritān / devāsura-manuṣyeṣu mad-bhaktācaritāni ca
verses.translation: One should take shelter of the holy places frequented by My saintly devotees and [follow the example of] the conduct of My devotees among the demigods, humans and demons.

verses.sanskrit: पृथक् सत्रेण वा मह्यं पर्व-यात्रा-महोत्सवान् । कारयेद् गीत-नृत्याद्यैर् महाराज-विभूतिभिः
verses.transliteration: pṛthak satreṇa vā mahyaṃ parva-yātrā-mahotsavān / kārayed gīta-nṛtyādyair mahārāja-vibhūtibhiḥ
verses.translation: Either alone or in association one should with respect for the position of the moon [e.g.], at special occasions and at festivals, engage in singing and dancing and so on, with royal opulence [and generous contributions].

verses.sanskrit: माम् एव सर्व-भूतेषु बहिर् अन्तर् अपावृतम् । ईक्षेतात्मनि चात्मानं यथा खम् अमलाशयः
verses.transliteration: mām eva sarva-bhūteṣu bahir antar apāvṛtam / īkṣetātmani cātmānaṃ yathā kham amalāśayaḥ
verses.translation: With a pure heart one should see Me, the Supreme Soul free as the sky, as being present within and without oneself and all living beings [see also B.G. 13: 16 and 1.7: 10].

verses.sanskrit: इति सर्वाणि भूतानि मद्-भावेन महा-द्युते । सभाजयन् मन्यमानो ज्ञानं केवलम् आश्रितः
verses.transliteration: iti sarvāṇi bhūtāni mad-bhāvena mahā-dyute / sabhājayan manyamāno jñānaṃ kevalam āśritaḥ
verses.translation: Oh brightest spirit, when one with My love thus is of respect for all living beings, one with such an approach has taken shelter of the highest possible knowledge, the absolute unity of spirit. This way regarding the brahmin and the outcast, the thief and the man faithful to the brahminical culture, the sun and the spark, the gentle one and the cruel one equally, one is considered a wise person [see B.G. 5: 18].

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणे पुक्कसे स्तेने ब्रह्मण्ये ऽर्के स्फुलिङ्गके । अक्रूरे क्रूरके चैव सम-दृक् पण्डितो मतः
verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇe pukkase stene brahmaṇye 'rke sphuliṅgake / akrūre krūrake caiva sama-dṛk paṇḍito mataḥ
verses.translation: Oh brightest spirit, when one with My love thus is of respect for all living beings, one with such an approach has taken shelter of the highest possible knowledge, the absolute unity of spirit. This way regarding the brahmin and the outcast, the thief and the man faithful to the brahminical culture, the sun and the spark, the gentle one and the cruel one equally, one is considered a wise person [see B.G. 5: 18].

verses.sanskrit: नरेष्व् अभीक्ष्णं मद्-भावं पुंसो भावयतो ऽचिरात् । स्पर्धासूया-तिरस्काराः साहङ्कारा वियन्ति हि
verses.transliteration: nareṣv abhīkṣṇaṃ mad-bhāvaṃ puṃso bhāvayato 'cirāt / spardhāsūyā-tiraskārāḥ sāhaṅkārā viyanti hi
verses.translation: Of the person who constantly meditates on My presence in all men, quickly the rivalry, envy, disdain and false ego will disappear.

verses.sanskrit: विसृज्य स्मयमानान् स्वान् दृशं व्रीडां च दैहिकीम् । प्रणमेद् दण्ड-वद् भूमाव् आ-श्व-चाण्डाल-गो-खरम्
verses.transliteration: visṛjya smayamānān svān dṛśaṃ vrīḍāṃ ca daihikīm / praṇamed daṇḍa-vad bhūmāv ā-śva-cāṇḍāla-go-kharam
verses.translation: Ignoring the laughter of one's friends and without being embarrassed about outer appearances, one should [factually] throw oneself like a rod to the ground and offer one's obeisances to [all,] even [to] dogs, outcasts, cows and asses [see also S'r S'r S'ikshshthaka-3].

verses.sanskrit: यावत् सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्-भावो नोपजायते । तावद् एवम् उपासीत वाङ्-मनः-काय-वृत्तिभिः
verses.transliteration: yāvat sarveṣu bhūteṣu mad-bhāvo nopajāyate / tāvad evam upāsīta vāṅ-manaḥ-kāya-vṛttibhiḥ
verses.translation: With the functions of speech, thought and action, one thus has to be of worship, for as long as one has not developed the vision of Me being present in all living beings [see also tridanda].

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं ब्रह्मात्मकं तस्य विद्ययात्म-मनीषया । परिपश्यन्न् उपरमेत् सर्वतो मुइत-संशयः
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ brahmātmakaṃ tasya vidyayātma-manīṣayā / paripaśyann uparamet sarvato muita-saṃśayaḥ
verses.translation: For the one who by knowledge and realization sees the Supreme Soul everywhere, everything is based upon the Absolute Truth. Thus free from doubt he desists [from activities so as to unite within].

verses.sanskrit: अयं हि सर्व-कल्पानां सध्रीचीनो मतो मम । मद्-भावः सर्व-भूतेषु मनो-वाक्-काय-वृत्तिभिः
verses.transliteration: ayaṃ hi sarva-kalpānāṃ sadhrīcīno mato mama / mad-bhāvaḥ sarva-bhūteṣu mano-vāk-kāya-vṛttibhiḥ
verses.translation: I consider this - with the functions of one's mind, words and actions seeing Me within all living beings - the most appropriate of all processes.

verses.sanskrit: न ह्य् अङ्गोपक्रमे ध्वंसो मद्-धर्मस्योद्धवाण्व् अपि । मया व्यवसितः सम्यङ् निर्गुणत्वाद् अनाशिषः
verses.transliteration: na hy aṅgopakrame dhvaṃso mad-dharmasyoddhavāṇv api / mayā vyavasitaḥ samyaṅ nirguṇatvād anāśiṣaḥ
verses.translation: My dear, because this by Me perfectly established method is free from the modes and has no ulterior motives, there is, when one thus tries to be of service unto Me, Uddhava, not even the slightest loss [see also B.G. 2: 40].

verses.sanskrit: यो यो मयि परे धर्मः कल्प्यते निष्फलाय चेत् । तद्-आयासो निरर्थः स्याद् भयादेर् इव सत्तम
verses.transliteration: yo yo mayi pare dharmaḥ kalpyate niṣphalāya cet / tad-āyāso nirarthaḥ syād bhayāder iva sattama
verses.translation: Oh best among the pious souls, when one is capable of performing one's duty free from ulterior motives for the sake of Me, the One Supreme, [the emotions] of that endeavor like fear and such, will be futile [see also B.G. 18: 6].

verses.sanskrit: एषा बुद्धिमतां बुद्धिर् मनीषा च मनीषिणाम् । यत् सत्यम् अनृतेनेह मर्त्येनाप्नोति मामृतम्
verses.transliteration: eṣā buddhimatāṃ buddhir manīṣā ca manīṣiṇām / yat satyam anṛteneha martyenāpnoti māmṛtam
verses.translation: This in one's life, by means of [desisting from] the deceptive and mortal, achieving of Me, the One Immortal, constitutes the cleverness of the clever and the intelligence of the intelligent.

verses.sanskrit: एष ते ऽभिहितः कृत्स्नो ब्रह्म-वादस्य सङ्ग्रहः । समास-व्यास-विधिना देवानाम् अपि दुर्गमः
verses.transliteration: eṣa te 'bhihitaḥ kṛtsno brahma-vādasya saṅgrahaḥ / samāsa-vyāsa-vidhinā devānām api durgamaḥ
verses.translation: This survey, I thus both in brief and in detail explained to you, constitutes the complete science of the Absolute Truth, that even for the demigods is difficult to access.

verses.sanskrit: अभीक्ष्णशस् ते गदितं ज्ञानं विस्पष्ट-युक्तिमत् । एतद् विज्ञाय मुच्येत पुरुषो नष्ट-संशयः
verses.transliteration: abhīkṣṇaśas te gaditaṃ jñānaṃ vispaṣṭa-yuktimat / etad vijñāya mucyeta puruṣo naṣṭa-saṃśayaḥ
verses.translation: With clear, logical arguments I repeatedly explained to you the spiritual knowledge; properly understood this will put an end to the doubts a person may have, and liberate him.

verses.sanskrit: सु-विविक्तं तव प्रश्नं मयैतद् अपि धारयेत् । सनातनं ब्रह्म-गुह्यं परं ब्रह्माधिगच्छति
verses.transliteration: su-viviktaṃ tava praśnaṃ mayaitad api dhārayet / sanātanaṃ brahma-guhyaṃ paraṃ brahmādhigacchati
verses.translation: He who concentrates on this question of yours as also on My clear reply, will attain the eternal secret of the Vedas, the Supreme, Absolute Truth.

verses.sanskrit: य एतन् मम भक्तेषु सम्प्रदद्यात् सु-पुष्कलम् । तस्याहं ब्रह्म-दायस्य ददाम्य् आत्मानम् आत्मना
verses.transliteration: ya etan mama bhakteṣu sampradadyāt su-puṣkalam / tasyāhaṃ brahma-dāyasya dadāmy ātmānam ātmanā
verses.translation: I shall give Myself to that person who, without reservation, passes on to devotees this traditional instruction of Mine, this knowledge of the Absolute Truth.

verses.sanskrit: य एतत् समधीयीत पवित्रं परमं शुचि । स पूयेताहर् अहर् मां ज्ञान-दीपेन दर्शयन्
verses.transliteration: ya etat samadhīyīta pavitraṃ paramaṃ śuci / sa pūyetāhar ahar māṃ jñāna-dīpena darśayan
verses.translation: He who repeats [for others] this Supreme [Knowledge] that is so sanctifying and clear, reveals My presence with the lamp of knowledge and will find purification day after day.

verses.sanskrit: य एतच् छ्रद्धया नित्यम् अव्यग्रः शृणुयान् नरः । मयि भक्तिं परां कुर्वन् कर्मभिर् न स बध्यते
verses.transliteration: ya etac chraddhayā nityam avyagraḥ śṛṇuyān naraḥ / mayi bhaktiṃ parāṃ kurvan karmabhir na sa badhyate
verses.translation: The person who attentively and with faith regularly listens to this and is of transcendental devotional service unto Me [is a bhakta], will not get entangled in karmic activities [see also B.G. 3: 9].

verses.sanskrit: अप्य् उद्धव त्वया ब्रह्म सखे समवधारितम् । अपि ते विगतो मोहः शोकश् चासौ मनो-भवः
verses.transliteration: apy uddhava tvayā brahma sakhe samavadhāritam / api te vigato mohaḥ śokaś cāsau mano-bhavaḥ
verses.translation: Uddhava, oh friend, do you have a clear understanding of the spiritual now, and has this lamentation and illusion that arose in your mind been removed [see 11.6: 42-49 and also B.G. 18: 72]?

verses.sanskrit: नैतत् त्वया दाम्भिकाय नास्तिकाय शठाय च । अशुश्रूषोर् अभक्ताय दुर्विनीताय दीयताम्
verses.transliteration: naitat tvayā dāmbhikāya nāstikāya śaṭhāya ca / aśuśrūṣor abhaktāya durvinītāya dīyatām
verses.translation: Do not share this with a hypocrite, an atheist or a cheat, nor with someone not willing to listen, a non-devotee or an obstinate person [compare to B.G. 18: 67].

verses.sanskrit: एतैर् दोषैर् विहीनाय ब्रह्मण्याय प्रियाय च । साधवे शुचये ब्रूयाद् भक्तिः स्याच् छूद्र-योषिताम्
verses.transliteration: etair doṣair vihīnāya brahmaṇyāya priyāya ca / sādhave śucaye brūyād bhaktiḥ syāc chūdra-yoṣitām
verses.translation: Share it with a person free from these bad qualities, someone virtuous and pure, kindly disposed and dedicated to the welfare of the brahmins, as also with laborers and women if they are of devotion [compare B.G. 9: 32].

verses.sanskrit: नैतद् विज्ञाय जिज्ञासोर् ज्ञातव्यम् अवशिष्यते । पीत्वा पीयूषम् अमृतं पातव्यं नावशिष्यते
verses.transliteration: naitad vijñāya jijñāsor jñātavyam avaśiṣyate / pītvā pīyūṣam amṛtaṃ pātavyaṃ nāvaśiṣyate
verses.translation: For the inquisitive one fully understanding this, there is nothing further to know; once one has drunk the nectar of this palatable beverage, nothing will remain.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञाने कर्मणि योगे च वार्तायां दण्ड-धारणे । यावान् अर्थो नृणां तात तावांस् ते ऽहं चतुर्-विधः
verses.transliteration: jñāne karmaṇi yoge ca vārtāyāṃ daṇḍa-dhāraṇe / yāvān artho nṛṇāṃ tāta tāvāṃs te 'haṃ catur-vidhaḥ
verses.translation: Everything that people of success with the four goals of life [catuh-vidhah] may find in knowledge, in fruitful labor, in mystical yoga, in ordinary activities or in political ruling, you can equally find in Me, My best one [see also B.G. 18: 66].

verses.sanskrit: मर्त्यो यदा त्यक्त-समस्त-कर्मा निवेदितात्मा विचिकीर्षितो मे । तदामृतत्वं प्रतिपद्यमानो मयात्म-भूयाय च कल्पते वै
verses.transliteration: martyo yadā tyakta-samasta-karmā niveditātmā vicikīrṣito me / tadāmṛtatvaṃ pratipadyamāno mayātma-bhūyāya ca kalpate vai
verses.translation: When a mortal surrenders himself to Me and forsakes all his self-interested labor in his desire of service, he at that time attains freedom from birth and death and qualifies for sharing in My nature.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-शुक उवाच स एवम् आदर्शित-योग-मार्गस् तदोत्तमःश्लोक-वचो निशम्य । बद्धाञ्जलिः प्रीत्य्-उपरुद्ध-कण्ठो न किञ्चिद् ऊचे ऽश्रु-परिप्लुताक्षः
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvāca sa evam ādarśita-yoga-mārgas tadottamaḥśloka-vaco niśamya / baddhāñjaliḥ prīty-uparuddha-kaṇṭho na kiñcid ūce 'śru-pariplutākṣaḥ
verses.translation: S'r S'uka said: 'After he had heard the words of Uttamas'loka and thus was shown the path of yoga, Uddhava with folded hands said nothing because his throat was choked up with love and his eyes were brimming with tears.

verses.sanskrit: विष्टभ्य चित्तं प्रणयावघूर्णं धैर्येण राजन् बहु-मन्यमानः । कृताञ्जलिः प्राह यदु-प्रवीरं शीर्ष्णा स्पृशंस् तच्-चरणारविन्दम्
verses.transliteration: viṣṭabhya cittaṃ praṇayāvaghūrṇaṃ dhairyeṇa rājan bahu-manyamānaḥ / kṛtāñjaliḥ prāha yadu-pravīraṃ śīrṣṇā spṛśaṃs tac-caraṇāravindam
verses.translation: Checking himself to steady his mind that was overwhelmed by love, oh King, he felt most grateful. With folded hands he touched the lotus feet of the Hero of the Yadus with his head and addressed Him.

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच विद्रावितो मोह-महान्धकारो य आश्रितो मे तव सन्निधानात् । विभावसोः किं नु समीप-गस्य शीतं तमो भीः प्रभवन्त्य् अजाद्य
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca vidrāvito moha-mahāndhakāro ya āśrito me tava sannidhānāt / vibhāvasoḥ kiṃ nu samīpa-gasya śītaṃ tamo bhīḥ prabhavanty ajādya
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'The great darkness of the delusion I embraced, oh Unborn Primeval Person, was dispelled by Your presence. What cold, darkness and fear would have power over someone who approached the sun?

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्यर्पितो मे भवतानुकम्पिना भृत्याय विज्ञान-मयः प्रदीपः । हित्वा कृत-ज्ञस् तव पाद-मूलं को ऽन्यं समीयाच् छरणं त्वदीयम्
verses.transliteration: pratyarpito me bhavatānukampinā bhṛtyāya vijñāna-mayaḥ pradīpaḥ / hitvā kṛta-jñas tava pāda-mūlaṃ ko 'nyaṃ samīyāc charaṇaṃ tvadīyam
verses.translation: You who are so merciful in Your goodness offered to me, Your servant, in return the torchlight consisting of Your wisdom. Whoever filled with gratitude can abandon the basis of Your feet and look for another shelter?

verses.sanskrit: वृक्णश् च मे सु-दृढः स्नेह-पाशो दाशार्ह-वृष्ण्य्-अन्धक-सात्वतेषु । प्रसारितः सृष्टि-विवृद्धये त्वया स्व-मायया ह्य् आत्म-सुबोध-हेतिना
verses.transliteration: vṛkṇaś ca me su-dṛḍhaḥ sneha-pāśo dāśārha-vṛṣṇy-andhaka-sātvateṣu / prasāritaḥ sṛṣṭi-vivṛddhaye tvayā sva-māyayā hy ātma-subodha-hetinā
verses.translation: The because of Your my firmly binding rope of my affection for the Ds'rhas, Vrishnis, Andhakas and Stvatas, cast by You for the production of offspring, was severed by the sword of the correct knowledge about the soul.

verses.sanskrit: नमो ऽस्तु ते महा-योगिन् प्रपन्नम् अनुशाधि माम् । यथा त्वच्-चरणाम्भोजे रतिः स्याद् अनपायिनी
verses.transliteration: namo 'stu te mahā-yogin prapannam anuśādhi mām / yathā tvac-caraṇāmbhoje ratiḥ syād anapāyinī
verses.translation: Let me offer my obeisances unto You, oh Greatest Yogi, please tell me how I as a surrendered soul can be steadfast in the attraction of Your lotus feet.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच गच्छोद्धव मयादिष्टो बदर्य्-आख्यं ममाश्रमम् । तत्र मत्-पाद-तीर्थोदे स्नानोपस्पर्शनैः शुचिः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca gacchoddhava mayādiṣṭo badary-ākhyaṃ mamāśramam / tatra mat-pāda-tīrthode snānopasparśanaiḥ śuciḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Please Uddhava, accept My advice to head for My hermitage called Badrika. At the riverbanks there be purified by the touching of and bathing in the water emanating from My feet [see 5.17]. Be, with your eyes fixed upon the Alakanand [a tributary of the Ganges] cleansed of all impurities, dress yourself in bark, My dear, eat from the forest and be happy freed from desire. Exercise with your intelligence, spiritual knowledge and wisdom, forbearance with all dualities, keep saintly to your principles, restrict your senses and live in peace and absorption. Believe in and meditate on that what you from Me have learned to discriminate. When you with your words and mind absorbed in Me thus devote yourself to My dharma, you will, with that discipline reaching beyond the three destinations [the three worlds], thereupon reach Me.'

verses.sanskrit: ईक्षयालकनन्दाया विधूताशेष-कल्मषः । वसानो वल्कलान्य् अङ्ग वन्य-भुक् सुख-निःस्पृहः
verses.transliteration: īkṣayālakanandāyā vidhūtāśeṣa-kalmaṣaḥ / vasāno valkalāny aṅga vanya-bhuk sukha-niḥspṛhaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Please Uddhava, accept My advice to head for My hermitage called Badrika. At the riverbanks there be purified by the touching of and bathing in the water emanating from My feet [see 5.17]. Be, with your eyes fixed upon the Alakanand [a tributary of the Ganges] cleansed of all impurities, dress yourself in bark, My dear, eat from the forest and be happy freed from desire. Exercise with your intelligence, spiritual knowledge and wisdom, forbearance with all dualities, keep saintly to your principles, restrict your senses and live in peace and absorption. Believe in and meditate on that what you from Me have learned to discriminate. When you with your words and mind absorbed in Me thus devote yourself to My dharma, you will, with that discipline reaching beyond the three destinations [the three worlds], thereupon reach Me.'

verses.sanskrit: तितिक्षुर् द्वन्द्व-मात्राणां सुशीलः संयतेन्द्रियः । शान्तः समाहित-धिया ज्ञान-विज्ञान-संयुतः
verses.transliteration: titikṣur dvandva-mātrāṇāṃ suśīlaḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ / śāntaḥ samāhita-dhiyā jñāna-vijñāna-saṃyutaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Please Uddhava, accept My advice to head for My hermitage called Badrika. At the riverbanks there be purified by the touching of and bathing in the water emanating from My feet [see 5.17]. Be, with your eyes fixed upon the Alakanand [a tributary of the Ganges] cleansed of all impurities, dress yourself in bark, My dear, eat from the forest and be happy freed from desire. Exercise with your intelligence, spiritual knowledge and wisdom, forbearance with all dualities, keep saintly to your principles, restrict your senses and live in peace and absorption. Believe in and meditate on that what you from Me have learned to discriminate. When you with your words and mind absorbed in Me thus devote yourself to My dharma, you will, with that discipline reaching beyond the three destinations [the three worlds], thereupon reach Me.'

verses.sanskrit: मत्तो ऽनुशिक्षितं यत् ते विविक्तम् अनुभावयन् । मय्य् आवेशित-वाक्-चित्तो मद्-धर्म-निरतो भव । अतिव्रज्य गतीस् तिस्रो माम् एष्यसि ततः परम्
verses.transliteration: matto 'nuśikṣitaṃ yat te viviktam anubhāvayan / mayy āveśita-vāk-citto mad-dharma-nirato bhava / ativrajya gatīs tisro mām eṣyasi tataḥ param
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Please Uddhava, accept My advice to head for My hermitage called Badrika. At the riverbanks there be purified by the touching of and bathing in the water emanating from My feet [see 5.17]. Be, with your eyes fixed upon the Alakanand [a tributary of the Ganges] cleansed of all impurities, dress yourself in bark, My dear, eat from the forest and be happy freed from desire. Exercise with your intelligence, spiritual knowledge and wisdom, forbearance with all dualities, keep saintly to your principles, restrict your senses and live in peace and absorption. Believe in and meditate on that what you from Me have learned to discriminate. When you with your words and mind absorbed in Me thus devote yourself to My dharma, you will, with that discipline reaching beyond the three destinations [the three worlds], thereupon reach Me.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-शुक उवाच स एवम् उक्तो हरि-मेधसोद्धवः प्रदक्षिणं तं परिसृत्य पादयोः । शिरो निधायाश्रु-कलाभिर् आर्द्र-धीर् न्यषिञ्चद् अद्वन्द्व-परो ऽप्य् अपक्रमे
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvāca sa evam ukto hari-medhasoddhavaḥ pradakṣiṇaṃ taṃ parisṛtya pādayoḥ / śiro nidhāyāśru-kalābhir ārdra-dhīr nyaṣiñcad advandva-paro 'py apakrame
verses.translation: S'r S'uka said: 'After thus having been addressed by the Lord of Understanding, Uddhava circumambulated Him keeping Him to the right, and even though he at the time of his departure was free from the influence of material opposites, he with a breaking heart with his head bowed down flooded His feet with his teardrops.

verses.sanskrit: सु-दुस्त्यज-स्नेह-वियोग-कातरो न शक्नुवंस् तं परिहातुम् आतुरः । कृच्छ्रं ययौ मूर्धनि भर्तृ-पादुके बिभ्रन् नमस्कृत्य ययौ पुनः पुनः
verses.transliteration: su-dustyaja-sneha-viyoga-kātaro na śaknuvaṃs taṃ parihātum āturaḥ / kṛcchraṃ yayau mūrdhani bhartṛ-pāduke bibhran namaskṛtya yayau punaḥ punaḥ
verses.translation: Finding it most difficult to let go of His love, he because of the departure was overwhelmed by emotions and could not abandon Him. Filled with pain he again and again offered his obeisances and placed the slippers of his Maintainer on his head. Then he departed [*].

verses.sanskrit: ततस् तम् अन्तर् हृदि सन्निवेश्य गतो महा-भागवतो विशालाम् । यथोपदिष्टां जगद्-एक-बन्धुना तपः समास्थाय हरेर् अगाद् गतिम्
verses.transliteration: tatas tam antar hṛdi sanniveśya gato mahā-bhāgavato viśālām / yathopadiṣṭāṃ jagad-eka-bandhunā tapaḥ samāsthāya harer agād gatim
verses.translation: The great devotee then installed Him permanently in his heart and went to the illustrious place of pilgrimage [which as such is also called Vis'l] the One Friend in the Universe had mentioned. There properly executing his austerities, he attained the Lord's destination [Vaikunthha].

verses.sanskrit: य एतद् आनन्द-समुद्र-सम्भृतं ज्ञानामृतं भागवताय भाषितम् । कृष्णेन योगेश्वर-सेविताङ्घ्रिणा सच्-छ्रद्धयासेव्य जगद् विमुच्यते
verses.transliteration: ya etad ānanda-samudra-sambhṛtaṃ jñānāmṛtaṃ bhāgavatāya bhāṣitam / kṛṣṇena yogeśvara-sevitāṅghriṇā sac-chraddhayāsevya jagad vimucyate
verses.translation: Anyone who with honest belief is full of attention for [listens to, speaks about and practices] this ocean of ecstasy, this nectarean sea of spiritual knowledge [of bhakti-yoga] that by Krishna, He whose feet are served by the masters of Yoga, was collected for His devotee, will liberate [himself and therewith] the entire world.

verses.sanskrit: भव-भयम् अपहन्तुं ज्ञान-विज्ञान-सारं निगम-कृद् उपजह्रे भृङ्ग-वद् वेद-सारम् अमृतम् उदधितश् चापाययद् भृत्य-वर्गान् पुरुषम् ऋषभम् आद्यं कृष्ण-संज्ञं नतो ऽस्मि
verses.transliteration: bhava-bhayam apahantuṃ jñāna-vijñāna-sāraṃ $ nigama-kṛd upajahre bhṛṅga-vad veda-sāram &amṛtam udadhitaś cāpāyayad bhṛtya-vargān % puruṣam ṛṣabham ādyaṃ kṛṣṇa-saṃjñaṃ nato 'smi
verses.translation: I am bowed down to the greatest and first of all beings, the personality named Krishna, who makes His many devotees drink the nectar from the [milk] ocean that is the essence of the Vedas, the essence of the spiritual knowledge and wisdom that He, as the author of the Vedas, like a bee delivered in order to take away the fear of material existence.'
